pr.title: Import additional scratches by compiler name
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Bulk import of additional scratch/decomp.me entries into config/GALE01/scratches.txt, apparently grouped or selected by compiler name. The PR added 3208 lines and removed 1 line, expanding the scratch index with many candidate matches, partial matches, duplicates, and metadata-rich entries for functions, labels, and unresolved Untitled scratches. The author explicitly warned that the import contains many false positives and stated that scratches pulled in but not belonging to Melee should later be marked as ERROR for their address.

key_files.role: Scratch/decomp.me index for GALE01; bulk-expanded with thousands of additional candidate scratches and metadata.
key_files.change_summary: Added 3208 lines and removed 1 line across 138 hunks.

what_changed: Added many new entries to config/GALE01/scratches.txt using the existing scratch-list format: name = status_or_percent:address; // metadata.
Imported entries include exact OK-like candidates already present nearby as well as partial percentages such as 99.69%, 14.29%, 2.17%, 1.09%, and 0.76%.
New scratch names include raw address names such as func_80003100, func_8000ADF4, func_8000AEC8, func_8000B074, lbl_8000B9D8, and lbl_8001615C.

New scratch names also include more descriptive or namespace-like names where available, such as __fill_mem, __check_pad3, __init_data, __init_hardware, lbvector_angle_xy_8000D620, lbArchive_InitializeDAT, lbArchive_80016AF0, lbFile_800161A0, lbrefract::atan2f, and Camera_80029BC4 in surrounding context.
Preserved/imported metadata fields in comments, including author, id, parent, updated timestamp, and created timestamp.
Allowed multiple scratches for the same address and symbol, including duplicate or parent-child chains at addresses such as 0x80003100, 0x800051EC, 0x8000AEC8, 0x8000C0E8, 0x800161A0, and 0x8001D350.
PR body notes that many imported entries are expected false positives and should be handled later rather than filtered at import time.
smart_moves: Kept the bulk import confined to a single config data file, avoiding source or build-system churn.
Retained scratch provenance metadata, making later cleanup, attribution, and parent-chain tracing possible.
Imported partial and low-confidence matches instead of only exact matches, which can expose useful failed attempts, compiler behavior clues, or near-match starting points for future decompilation.
Explicitly documented expected false positives in the PR body, reducing ambiguity for future maintainers who encounter suspicious entries.
Established a cleanup convention: scratches that were pulled but do not belong to Melee should be marked as ERROR for their address.
decomp_lessons: Bulk scratch imports are useful as discovery data, but should be treated as a candidate index rather than trusted matching evidence.
False positives in scratch/config data should be preserved with an explicit ERROR marker when identified, rather than silently deleting provenance.
Multiple scratches for one address can be valuable because parent/child relationships and competing attempts may reveal different matching tactics.
Percent-match entries can be useful even when far from matching, but downstream users should prioritize OK or high-percentage entries and verify addresses manually.
For decomp workflow records, preserving author, scratch id, parent id, and timestamps is valuable searchable metadata.
naming_conventions: Raw unmatched functions use address-derived names such as func_80003100 and func_8000AEC8.
Raw labels use lbl_XXXXXXXX naming, as seen with lbl_8000B9D8 and lbl_8001615C.
Some library or subsystem names prefix the address, such as lbArchive_80016AF0, lbFile_800161A0, lbMemory_800154BC, lbHeap_800158D0, lbGx_8001E2F8, and Camera_80029BC4.
Some C/runtime or low-level symbols use double-underscore names, such as __fill_mem, __check_pad3, __init_data, __init_hardware, and __flush_cache.
Some scratch names preserve namespace-like syntax, such as lbrefract::atan2f and lbrefract::func_80022D1C.
Unidentified imported scratches may remain named Untitled until a better symbol or ownership is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly rewrite or source matching tactic was present; this PR was a data/config import.

The matching-relevant tactic was to collect scratches by compiler name, likely to surface candidates compiled with compatible settings, but the dump provides no detailed compiler list or filtering algorithm.
The scratch status format records a rough match quality as either OK, TODO, ERROR, or a percentage tied to an address, enabling future triage by confidence.
Parent scratch IDs in comments provide a way to trace iterative matching attempts and compare variants.
review_feedback: There were no issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the provided PR slice.
Author note: the import contains many false positives, which can be dealt with later.
Author guidance: scratches pulled in but not belonging to Melee should be marked as ERROR for their address.
searchable_terms: Import additional scratches by compiler name
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mark imported scratches as ERROR in config/GALE01/scratches.txt.
Search for uses of parent scratch IDs imported in PR 1211 to identify which candidates became accepted matches.
Look for later cleanup of false positives introduced by the compiler-name scratch import.
Compare entries with low match percentages against later OK entries for the same address.
Investigate tooling or scripts that generated scratches.txt entries by compiler name.
